source_pr.title: planner/core: fix bug when using generated column in aggregate statement
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Fix #14072 

### What is changed and how it works?

When checking if aggregate operator can be push-down, it should also check that if `groupByItem` contained generated columns, if true, current aggregate operator should not be push-down.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Integration test

Code changes

 - None

Side effects

 - None
Related changes

 - None

Release note

 - None

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select sum(a) from t1 group by b order by b;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "3", "2", "2")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select sum(a) from t1 group by b order by b;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "3", "2", "2")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes a planner correctness bug (generated columns in aggregate pushdown) and adds regression tests. It does not include a flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization mechanism, so avoid guessing a flakiness root cause.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select sum(a) from t1 group by b order by b;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "3", "2", "2")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
